title: Space Tech Briefing — June 02, 2026
summary:
Good morning, space enthusiasts! It's June 02, 2026, and you are locked into your daily dose of cosmic news right here, where we bring you the latest from beyond our blue planet! Today, we have some absolutely stellar news that's buzzing across the globe!

Get ready for a major announcement! The President of the French Republic has just revealed a groundbreaking agreement with the American company Vast. Vast, for those who don't know, is pioneering the development of the very first commercial space station. And this deal? It's set to send two French astronauts into orbit!

Yes, you heard that right! Our very own space heroes, Thomas Pesquet and Arnaud Prost, are slated to participate in two distinct private missions in 2027. This incredible news was unveiled during the 'Choose France' summit, a key event for attracting foreign investment to France.

Let's dive into the details! Thomas Pesquet, a familiar face to many, is expected to join the International Space Station – the ISS – on Vast's first private mission. Meanwhile, Arnaud Prost is set to make history by participating in the inaugural mission to Vast's brand-new private station, named Haven-1. Imagine that! A pioneering flight to a new commercial outpost in space!

This strategic agreement is a massive win for France. It ensures a continued, active French presence in low Earth orbit. And it positions France right at the forefront, partnering with a visionary project in private space station development. It's truly a testament to the evolving landscape of space exploration, where international collaboration meets commercial innovation.

What an exciting time for space exploration! Thomas Pesquet and Arnaud Prost, leading the charge into a new era of private spaceflight. We'll be keeping a close eye on these missions as they develop. For now, that's your morning briefing from the cosmos. Stay curious, keep looking up, and have a fantastic day!
